title: Q1 2026 Infrastructure Review
subtitle: Quarterly Performance and Incident Summary
executive_summary: Infrastructure availability exceeded targets in Q1 2026, with overall uptime of 99.94% across production services. Two P1 incidents occurred during the quarter, both resolved within SLA. Cloud spend came in 4% under budget due to reserved instance optimization completed in February. The team deployed 847 changes to production, up 12% from Q4 2025, with zero rollbacks attributed to infrastructure failures.

incidents.description: Database connection pool exhaustion in the payments service caused transaction failures for approximately 2,400 customers during peak hours.
incidents.root_cause: Connection leak in the retry logic introduced in deploy #712. Connections were not being returned to the pool on timeout exceptions.
incidents.resolution: Hotfix deployed to correct connection handling. Added connection pool monitoring alerts.

incidents.description: DNS propagation failure during planned migration from legacy nameservers caused intermittent resolution failures across all public-facing services.
incidents.root_cause: TTL values on legacy records were set to 24 hours instead of the planned 5 minutes, causing stale records to persist in downstream caches.
incidents.resolution: Manual cache flush coordinated with CDN provider. DNS migration playbook updated with TTL pre-flight checklist.

recommendations: Migrate remaining on-demand compute workloads to reserved instances (est. savings: $8,400/quarter).
Implement automated S3 lifecycle policies for log archives older than 90 days.
Complete the transition to the new DNS provider before Q2 maintenance window.
Add connection pool exhaustion to the standard pre-deploy health check suite.
